event: presented to a medical center in India
CT scan of the abdomen revealed chronic atrophic calcific pancreatitis
ERCP confirmed dilated pancreatic duct and intraductal calculi
hospitalized for pain control with narcotics
CT scan revealed fatty atrophy of both the pancreatic head and uncinate process
pancreatic duct filled with large calcifications
possible obstruction at the level of the neck
marked upstream ductal dilatation of 1.5 cm
admitted for management of pain secondary to chronic pancreatitis
transferred to the medical intensive care unit
major papilla of Vater spontaneously expelling frank pus
more than 5 ml of yellow pus evacuated
fluid could not be aspirated for culture
cholangiogram revealed a normal biliary tree
pancreatogram showed marked dilatation of the main pancreatic duct
guide wire introduced into the pancreatic duct
5-cm-long 5 F stent placed into the pancreatic duct
acute suppuration of the pancreatic duct
contrast enhanced CT scan of the abdomen
inflammatory changes within the fat surrounding the body and tail of the pancreas
dilatation of the pancreatic duct diminished
calculus showing distal migration towards the sphincter of Oddi
Klebsiella ornithinolytica sensitive to the antibiotics given
transferred from the intensive care unit
